title: Parkio Daily for May 16, 2026
teaser: New Mandalorian missions land on Smugglers Run, Grogu projection show debuts at Disneyland, and Muppets Rock 'n' Roller Coaster preview arrives.

rides.name: Big Thunder Mountain Railroad
rides.note: Reopened after refurbishment—morning waits are lowest

rides.name: Soarin' Across America
rides.note: New version just opened for cast preview—light crowds expected

rides.name: Millennium Falcon: Smugglers Run
rides.note: New Mandalorian mission launches today—ride early before word spreads

breaking.title: New Mandalorian & Grogu Mission Launches on Millennium Falcon: Smugglers Run Today
breaking.body: Starting today, Smugglers Run at both Walt Disney World and Disneyland features an all-new storyline where guests team up with Din Djarin and Grogu to chase ex-Imperial officers and pirates across Tatooine. Engineers can now interact with Grogu and choose which planet to visit—Bespin, Endor, or Coruscant—adding replay value to each flight.

parkioInsight.text: Galaxy's Edge tends to see surge crowds when major updates drop. Rope-dropping Smugglers Run or visiting after 8 p.m. usually offers shorter waits today.

breaking.title: Grogu Projection Show Debuts Tonight at Disneyland's Galaxy's Edge
breaking.body: The Curious Child, a limited-time nighttime projection show starring Grogu, begins tonight at Disneyland. The show plays after Shadows of Memory on the rock spires near the Millennium Falcon, featuring moments from Grogu's adventures with the Mandalorian ahead of the film's May 22 release.

parkioInsight.text: Nighttime projection shows in Galaxy's Edge usually draw smaller crowds than main fireworks. Arriving 15 minutes early near the Falcon often secures good sightlines without a long wait.

bignews.title: Muppets Rock 'n' Roller Coaster Previews Reveal New Animatronics and Vehicles
bignews.body: Disney gave a first look at Rock 'n' Roller Coaster Starring The Muppets, showing three new Audio-Animatronics in the preshow, updated ride vehicles with fresh on-ride photo tech, and a giant guitar out front. New merchandise including t-shirts, figurines, and collectibles is now available at Hollywood Studios ahead of the ride's full reopening.

parkioInsight.text: The new Muppets merch tends to sell out fast in the first weeks at flagship Hollywood Studios stores. Visiting Rock 'n' Roller Coaster's gift shop early in the day may improve selection.

bignews.title: New Star Wars Food and Merch Roll Out Across Both Coasts
bignews.body: Docking Bay 7 at both resorts now serves Grogu Cookies and Sweet-and-Spicy Puffer Pig Pasta (Walt Disney World only). The Mandalorian and Grogu Jetpack Sipper and BDX Droid Bucket are available parkwide, with a two-per-person limit. Themed apparel, puzzles, and figure sets are stocked at Star Wars Galactic Outpost and Tatooine Traders.

parkioInsight.text: Limited-edition sippers and buckets often run out by midday on launch day. Grabbing one first thing or during late evening hours may avoid disappointment.

topstories.title: Soarin' Across America Opens for Cast Member Previews at EPCOT
topstories.body: The reimagined Soarin' Across America is now running cast previews at EPCOT ahead of its public debut. The updated film features new scenes across the United States, replacing the previous California-focused version that returned briefly in recent years.

parkioInsight.text: Soft openings and previews typically mean lighter crowds and shorter waits. If Soarin' is running for guests today, riding in the first hour after park open usually avoids longer afternoon lines.

topstories.title: Starbucks Adds Three Summer Menu Items Nationwide
topstories.body: Starbucks launched its summer menu today with a Tropical Butterfly Lemonade Refresher, Butterfly Drink, and Horchata Frappuccino. All three are available at Starbucks locations in Disney Springs, Disneyland's Downtown Disney, and inside the parks.

parkioInsight.text: Starbucks locations inside the parks tend to have longer lines mid-morning. Stopping at Downtown Disney or Disney Springs before or after park hours often cuts wait times in half.

icymi.title: Disneyland Files Permits for Shopping Complex on Toy Story Lot
icymi.body: New construction permits filed for the former Toy Story parking area indicate plans for a shopping and dining complex, not a third theme park as some fans had speculated. The project is part of Disneyland's broader DisneylandForward expansion plan.

icymi.title: Disney CFO Says Parks Are at Capacity Despite Softer Attendance Indicators
icymi.body: At an industry conference, Disney CFO Hugh Johnston stated the parks are "filled up" and cannot add more guests. However, wait times, promotional discounts, and attendance trends suggest capacity is not the primary constraint on growth.

spotlight.title: The Mandalorian and Grogu Movie Premieres Next Week—Park Tie-Ins Are Live Now
spotlight.body: With Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu hitting theaters May 22, Disney has rolled out new attraction updates, nighttime shows, food, and merchandise across Galaxy's Edge at both coasts. The wave of new content marks one of the biggest Star Wars park activations since the land's 2019 opening.

parkioInsight.text: Film tie-in weeks usually draw higher-than-normal crowds to themed lands. Visiting Galaxy's Edge on weekday mornings or after 7 p.m. tends to offer better breathing room and shorter attraction waits.

videos.title: I Tested the WORST Rated Restaurant in Magic Kingdom- The Plaza
videos.summary: A creator visits The Plaza Restaurant on Main Street U.S.A., which has a reputation as one of Magic Kingdom's lowest-rated dining spots, to see if the reviews are justified.
